en: Then the whole company of them arose and brought him before Pilate.
zh: 眾人都起來，把耶穌押到彼拉多那裡，

en: And they began to accuse him, saying, "We found this man misleading our nation and forbidding us to give tribute to Caesar, and saying that he himself is Christ, a king."
zh: 控告他說：“我們查出這個人煽惑我們的同胞，阻止納稅給凱撒，並且自稱是基督，是王。”

en: And Pilate asked him, "Are you the King of the Jews?" And he answered him, "You have said so."
zh: 彼拉多問他：“你是猶太人的王嗎？”耶穌回答：“你已經說了 。”

en: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, "I find no guilt in this man."
zh: 彼拉多對祭司長和眾人說：“我在這人身上，查不出有甚麼罪。”

en: But they were urgent, saying, "He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, from Galilee even to this place."
zh: 但他們極力說：“他在猶太全地教導人，煽動群眾，從加利利直到這裡。”

en: When Pilate heard this, he asked whether the man was a Galilean.
zh: 彼拉多聽見了，就問耶穌是不是加利利人。

en: And when he learned that he belonged to Herod's jurisdiction, he sent him over to Herod, who was himself in Jerusalem at that time.
zh: 既然知道他是屬於希律管轄的，就把他送回希律那裡；那時希律正在耶路撒冷。

en: When Herod saw Jesus, he was very glad, for he had long desired to see him, because he had heard about him, and he was hoping to see some sign done by him.
zh: 希律看見耶穌，非常歡喜，因為他曾經聽過耶穌的事，早就想要見他，希望看他行個神蹟。

en: So he questioned him at some length, but he made no answer.
zh: 於是他問了耶穌許多話，但耶穌甚麼也不回答。

en: The chief priests and the scribes stood by, vehemently accusing him.
zh: 祭司長和經學家站著，猛烈地控告他。

en: And Herod with his soldiers treated him with contempt and mocked him. Then, arraying him in splendid clothing, he sent him back to Pilate.
zh: 希律和他的侍衛就藐視耶穌，戲弄他，給他穿上華麗的衣服，把他送回彼拉多那裡。

en: And Herod and Pilate became friends with each other that very day, for before this they had been at enmity with each other.
zh: 希律和彼拉多從前原是彼此為仇，在那一天就成了朋友。

en: Pilate then called together the chief priests and the rulers and the people,
zh: 彼拉多召集了祭司長、官長和民眾，

en: and said to them, "You brought me this man as one who was misleading the people. And after examining him before you, behold, I did not find this man guilty of any of your charges against him.
zh: 對他們說：“你們把這人押到我這裡來，說他煽惑群眾，我已經在你們面前審訊過，在他身上一點也找不到你們控告他的罪狀，

en: Neither did Herod, for he sent him back to us. Look, nothing deserving death has been done by him.
zh: 連希律也找不到，又把他送回我這裡，可見他沒有作過該死的事。

en: I will therefore punish and release him."
zh: 我要責打他，然後把他釋放。”

en: But they all cried out together, "Away with this man, and release to us Barabbas"-
zh: 眾人齊聲喊叫：“除掉這個人，給我們釋放巴拉巴！”

en: a man who had been thrown into prison for an insurrection started in the city and for murder.
zh: 這巴拉巴是因為在城裡作亂殺人而入獄的。

en: Pilate addressed them once more, desiring to release Jesus,
zh: 彼拉多再向他們說明，願意釋放耶穌。

en: but they kept shouting, "Crucify, crucify him!"
zh: 然而他們高聲呼叫：“把他釘十字架，把他釘十字架！”

en: A third time he said to them, "Why, what evil has he done? I have found in him no guilt deserving death. I will therefore punish and release him."
zh: 彼拉多第三次對他們說：“這人作過甚麼惡事呢？我在他身上找不出甚麼該死的罪。所以我要責打他，然後把他釋放。”

en: But they were urgent, demanding with loud cries that he should be crucified. And their voices prevailed.
zh: 但他們大聲吵鬧，要他把耶穌釘十字架，他們的聲音就得了勝。

en: So Pilate decided that their demand should be granted.
zh: 彼拉多就宣判，照他們的要求，

en: He released the man who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, for whom they asked, but he delivered Jesus over to their will.
zh: 把他們所求那作亂殺人入獄的釋放了，卻把耶穌交出來，隨他們的意思處理。

en: And as they led him away, they seized one Simon of Cyrene, who was coming in from the country, and laid on him the cross, to carry it behind Jesus.
zh: 他們把耶穌帶走的時候，抓住了一個從鄉下來的古利奈人西門，把十字架放在他身上，叫他背著跟在耶穌後面。

en: And there followed him a great multitude of the people and of women who were mourning and lamenting for him.
zh: 一大群人跟隨他，有些婦女為他捶胸痛哭。

en: But turning to them Jesus said, "Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.
zh: 耶穌轉過身來對她們說：“耶路撒冷的女兒啊，不要為我哭，卻要為你們自己和你們的兒女哭。

en: For behold, the days are coming when they will say, 'Blessed are the barren and the wombs that never bore and the breasts that never nursed!'
zh: 日子將到，人必說：‘不生育的和沒有懷過胎的，也沒有哺養過嬰兒的有福了。’

en: Then they will begin to say to the mountains, 'Fall on us,' and to the hills, 'Cover us.'
zh: 那時人要對大山說： ‘倒在我們身上！’ 對小山說： ‘遮蓋我們！’

en: For if they do these things when the wood is green, what will happen when it is dry?"
zh: 他們在青綠的樹上，既然這樣作；在枯乾的樹上，又會怎樣呢？”

en: Two others, who were criminals, were led away to be put to death with him.
zh: 他們另外帶來兩個犯人，和耶穌一同處死，

en: And when they came to the place that is called The Skull, there they crucified him, and the criminals, one on his right and one on his left.
zh: 到了那名叫“髑髏”的地方，就把耶穌釘在十字架上，也釘了那兩個犯人，一左一右。

en: And Jesus said, "Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do." And they cast lots to divide his garments.
zh: 耶穌說：“父啊，赦免他們！因為他們不知道自己所作的是甚麼。”士兵抽籤，分了他的衣服。

en: And the people stood by, watching, but the rulers scoffed at him, saying, "He saved others; let him save himself, if he is the Christ of God, his Chosen One!"
zh: 群眾站著觀看，官長們嗤笑說：“他救了別人，如果他是基督，是 神所揀選的，讓他救自己吧！”

en: The soldiers also mocked him, coming up and offering him sour wine
zh: 士兵也上前戲弄他，拿酸酒給他喝，

en: and saying, "If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!"
zh: 說：“如果你是猶太人的王，救你自己吧！”

en: There was also an inscription over him, "This is the King of the Jews."
zh: 在耶穌的頭以上有一個牌子寫著：“這是猶太人的王。”

en: One of the criminals who were hanged railed at him, saying, "Are you not the Christ? Save yourself and us!"
zh: 懸掛著的犯人中，有一個侮辱他說：“你不是基督嗎？救你自己和我們吧！”

en: But the other rebuked him, saying, "Do you not fear God, since you are under the same sentence of condemnation?
zh: 另一個就應聲責備他說：“你是同樣受刑的，還不懼怕 神嗎？

en: And we indeed justly, for we are receiving the due reward of our deeds; but this man has done nothing wrong."
zh: 我們是罪有應得的。我們所受的與所作的相稱，然而這個人並沒有作過甚麼不對的事。”

en: And he said, "Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom."
zh: 他又對耶穌說：“耶穌啊，你得國降臨的時候，求你記念我。”

en: And he said to him, "Truly, I say to you, today you will be with me in Paradise."
zh: 耶穌對他說：“我實在告訴你，今天你必定同我在樂園裡了。”

en: It was now about the sixth hour, and there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour,
zh: 從大約正午直到下午三點鐘，遍地都黑暗了。

en: while the sun's light failed. And the curtain of the temple was torn in two.
zh: 太陽沒有光，聖所的幔子從當中裂開。

en: Then Jesus, calling out with a loud voice, said, "Father, into your hands I commit my spirit!" And having said this he breathed his last.
zh: 耶穌大聲呼叫：“父啊，我把我的靈魂交在你手裡。”說了這話，氣就斷了。

en: Now when the centurion saw what had taken place, he praised God, saying, "Certainly this man was innocent!"
zh: 百夫長看見所發生的事，就頌讚 神，說：“這真是個義人！”

en: And all the crowds that had assembled for this spectacle, when they saw what had taken place, returned home beating their breasts.
zh: 聚集觀看的群眾，看見所發生的事，都捶著胸回去了。

en: And all his acquaintances and the women who had followed him from Galilee stood at a distance watching these things.
zh: 與耶穌熟悉的人，和從加利利跟隨他來的婦女，都遠遠地站著，看這些事。

en: Now there was a man named Joseph, from the Jewish town of Arimathea. He was a member of the council, a good and righteous man,
zh: 有一個人名叫約瑟，是個議員，為人良善公義，

en: who had not consented to their decision and action; and he was looking for the kingdom of God.
zh: 是猶太地亞利馬太城的人，一向等候 神的國，並不附和眾人的計謀和行為。

en: This man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
zh: 這人去見彼拉多，求領耶穌的身體。

en: Then he took it down and wrapped it in a linen shroud and laid him in a tomb cut in stone, where no one had ever yet been laid.
zh: 他把身體取下來，用細麻布裹好，放在從石頭鑿出來的墳墓裡，這墳墓是從來沒有葬過人的。

en: It was the day of Preparation, and the Sabbath was beginning.
zh: 那天是預備日，安息日就要開始，

en: The women who had come with him from Galilee followed and saw the tomb and how his body was laid.
zh: 那些從加利利和耶穌一起來的婦女，跟著來了。她們看見了墳墓，和他的身體怎樣安葬，

en: Then they returned and prepared spices and ointments. On the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.
zh: 就回去預備香料和香膏。 安息日，她們遵著誡命安息。
